title: B-NLS: low-density 'hole' — quantum pressure singularity stress test
ic: N(x,0) = sech^2(x-5) + 0.001 (a sech^2 soliton plus tiny background — so N is small at x=-5 region). phi(x,0) = 0.1*x. u(x,0) = 0.1*N(x,0). Where N is small, (sqrt(N))_xx / sqrt(N) becomes singular numerically.
ground_truth: Unknown. The system may evolve smoothly (if quantum pressure is well-handled) or develop singular behavior.
stress_question: How small can N get before the chosen method fails? Madelung-Psi should handle this well (Psi=sqrt(N+0.001)*e^{i*phi} stays bounded); direct (N, phi) almost certainly fails. Where does each method break?
parameters_to_sweep: Try Madelung-Psi vs direct (N, phi). For direct, try regularizations eps in {1e-6, 1e-3}. Report min_N over the run for each.
